narrative.headline: 2020 Mazda CX-5: electrical complaints lead, but the brakes look most serious
narrative.summary: Owners filed 104 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 29%, followed by the engine (15%) and the brakes (13%). No fires and no deaths are reported across any area. The brakes stand out by outcome: just 14 reports, but they account for 6 crashes and 4 injuries, more than any other area, so they are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 30 reports (29%), with 1 crash and 1 injury. Watch for warning lights and electrical glitches, including some tied to braking and steering systems.

issues.sample: Currently own a 2020 Mazda CX 5 Touring, after coming from work I parked in my driveway and proceeded to turn off the car. When i was walking away i could hear the engine still Running. I went back and turned on the car and turned it back off, again i could still hear the engine
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 16 reports (15%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. A recurring complaint area but no serious outcomes reported.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Only 14 reports (13%) but the most serious by outcome: 6 crashes and 4 injuries, the highest of any area. Test the brakes thoroughly, including any sudden or unexpected braking.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 11 reports (11%), linked to 2 crashes and no injuries. Check that the driver-assist and collision systems behave predictably.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The fewest of the top five: 8 reports (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for jerking or rough shifting when accelerating.
